CALDWELL, Idaho, March 81
Attorneys for the Western Fedora
ton of Miners are under the search
light of the grand jury. Edward


Boyce, organizer of the federation
and its first president, now a
wealthy mine owner is under sur
veilance by private detectives. w.

tives, told the grand jury he be-
lieved 1t impossible to break up the
ring of dynamiters and eliminate
lawlessness among the miners I'M
less he could get at the moving
spirits and punish them. The jur-
ors questioned Swain as to whether
he had any evidence that publish
ers were affiliated with. the fedora
ton. He said he believed Simpkins
would be captured inside Of three
days as detectives were hot on his
